name: Chocolate Rum Espresso Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 20 amaretti macaroons or 3/4 cup graham cracker crumbs
2 1/2 ounces (1/2 cup) blanched, toasted almonds or hazelnuts
1/2 cup rum (or 3/4 to 1 cup Frangelico or Irish cream)
2 pounds cream cheese at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Adjust a rack one-third up from the bottom of the oven and preheat the oven to 375°F.
2. Process macaroons in a food processor until fine. Transfer crumbs to bowl; mix nuts and sugar until fine. Mix with macaroon crumbs, melted butter, stir to moisten evenly.
3. Spread crumbs evenly over bottom of 9-inch springform pan; press firmly to form crust. Bake for 8 minutes, then set aside to cool. When cool, refrigerate to chill the crust.
4. Melt chocolate in top of double boiler until smooth. Pour over chilled crust and spread with spoon, stopping 1/4 inch from edge. Brush melted butter onto sides; return to freezer.
5. Preheat oven to 350°F.
6. Cut chocolate for filling into small pieces; melt and stir smooth. Remove from double boiler and set aside.
7. Scald half the cream over medium heat. When skin forms, add cocoa and espresso powder. Whisk until smooth, cooking a few minutes stirring constantly until slightly thickened. Stir in rum and remaining cream.
8. Beat cream cheese until soft and fluffy; scrape beaters once during process. Add sugar and beat, scraping bowl occasionally until smooth. Add chocolate and then cream mixture while beating on low speed. Continue beating until completely smooth, scraping bowl occasionally. Add eggs and beat just until incorporated.
9. Remove pan from freezer; wrap in two layers of aluminum foil. Place inside larger shallow pan; pour filling into pan, rotating gently to level. Return to oven; before closing door, add 1/2 to 1 inch water to larger pan.
10. Bake cheesecake for one hour (cake will be liquid but still set). Turn off oven and let cake stand in oven with door slightly open for an hour.
11. Remove from oven; remove springform pan; unwrap foil; allow to cool to room temperature. Chill overnight before cutting.
12. Glaze with melted seedless fruit preserves, reducing slightly. Brush glaze over top and sides of cake. Serve topped with whipped cream. Use any liqueur desired; adjust amount according to strength. Generally espresso powder is omitted as flavors complement each other well. Alcoholic flavor may be replaced with espresso powder if desired; increase cream accordingly.
13. Beat cream cheese until light and fluffy for best texture.
